question: Which leads have upcoming appointments?
generated_sql:
WITH ranked_upcoming_leads AS (
  SELECT
    l.id AS lead_id,
    a.id AS appointment_id,
    a.schedule_time,
    COALESCE(
      NULLIF(TRIM(l.full_name), ''),
      NULLIF(TRIM(CONCAT_WS(' ', l.first_name, l.last_name)), ''),
      l.first_name,
      'Unknown Lead'
    ) AS display_name,
    COALESCE(NULLIF(TRIM(a.snapshot_event_name), ''), aet.event_type_name, 'Unknown Event Type') AS event_name,
    CAST(a.snapshot_call_category AS text) AS call_category,
    CAST(a.source AS text) AS appointment_source,
    COALESCE(NULLIF(TRIM(a.host_id), ''), 'No Host') AS host_id,
    COALESCE(NULLIF(TRIM(a.setter_id), ''), 'No Setter') AS setter_id,
    ROW_NUMBER() OVER (
      PARTITION BY l.id
      ORDER BY a.schedule_time ASC, a.id ASC
    ) AS rn
  FROM appointments a
  JOIN leads l
    ON l.id = a.lead_id
   AND l.clerk_org_id = a.clerk_org_id
   AND l.is_deleted = false
  LEFT JOIN appointment_event_types aet
    ON aet.id = a.appointment_event_type_id
   AND aet.clerk_org_id = a.clerk_org_id
   AND aet.is_deleted = false
  WHERE a.clerk_org_id = :org_id
    AND a.is_deleted = false
    AND a.schedule_time >= NOW()
)
SELECT
  COUNT(*) OVER() AS total_matching_rows,
  lead_id,
  display_name,
  appointment_id,
  schedule_time AS next_appointment_time,
  event_name,
  call_category,
  appointment_source,
  host_id,
  setter_id
FROM ranked_upcoming_leads
WHERE rn = 1
ORDER BY next_appointment_time ASC, appointment_id ASC
LIMIT :limit

  COALESCE(NULLIF(TRIM(a.snapshot_event_name), ''), aet.event_type_name, 'Unknown Event Type') AS event_name,
  CAST(a.snapshot_call_category AS text) AS call_category,
  COALESCE(outcome.name, 'No Outcome') AS outcome_name,
  COALESCE(CAST(outcome.role AS text), 'NO_OUTCOME') AS outcome_role,
  a.no_show,
  CAST(a.source AS text) AS appointment_source,
  COALESCE(NULLIF(TRIM(a.host_id), ''), 'No Host') AS host_id,
  COALESCE(NULLIF(TRIM(a.setter_id), ''), 'No Setter') AS setter_id,
  EXISTS (
    SELECT 1
    FROM fathom_call_records f_check
    WHERE f_check.appointment_id = a.id
      AND f_check.clerk_org_id = a.clerk_org_id
  ) AS has_fathom_record
FROM appointments a
LEFT JOIN leads l
  ON l.id = a.lead_id
 AND l.clerk_org_id = a.clerk_org_id
 AND l.is_deleted = false
LEFT JOIN sales_statuses outcome
  ON outcome.id = a.outcome_id
 AND outcome.clerk_org_id = a.clerk_org_id
LEFT JOIN appointment_event_types aet
  ON aet.id = a.appointment_event_type_id
 AND aet.clerk_org_id = a.clerk_org_id
 AND aet.is_deleted = false
WHERE a.clerk_org_id = :org_id
  AND a.is_deleted = false
  AND a.schedule_time < NOW()
  AND a.no_show = false
ORDER BY a.schedule_time DESC, a.id ASC
LIMIT 20

question: Which calls already happened?
notes: Past appointment count uses schedule_time < NOW(); attended/completed non-no-show uses no_show = false.
generated_sql:
SELECT
  COUNT(*) OVER() AS total_matching_rows,
  a.id,
  a.schedule_time,
  COALESCE(
    NULLIF(TRIM(l.full_name), ''),
    NULLIF(TRIM(CONCAT_WS(' ', l.first_name, l.last_name)), ''),
    l.first_name,
    'Unknown Lead'
  ) AS display_name,
  COALESCE(NULLIF(TRIM(a.snapshot_event_name), ''), aet.event_type_name, 'Unknown Event Type') AS event_name,
  CAST(a.snapshot_call_category AS text) AS call_category,
  COALESCE(outcome.name, 'No Outcome') AS outcome_name,
  a.no_show,
  CAST(a.source AS text) AS appointment_source
FROM appointments a
LEFT JOIN leads l
  ON l.id = a.lead_id
 AND l.clerk_org_id = a.clerk_org_id
 AND l.is_deleted = false
LEFT JOIN sales_statuses outcome
  ON outcome.id = a.outcome_id
 AND outcome.clerk_org_id = a.clerk_org_id
LEFT JOIN appointment_event_types aet
  ON aet.id = a.appointment_event_type_id
 AND aet.clerk_org_id = a.clerk_org_id
 AND aet.is_deleted = false
WHERE a.clerk_org_id = :org_id
  AND a.is_deleted = false
  AND a.schedule_time < NOW()
ORDER BY a.schedule_time DESC, a.id ASC
LIMIT :limit

question: Show me attended calls only.
notes: Past appointment count uses schedule_time < NOW(); attended/completed non-no-show uses no_show = false.
generated_sql:
SELECT
  COUNT(*) OVER() AS total_matching_rows,
  a.id,
  a.schedule_time,
  COALESCE(
    NULLIF(TRIM(l.full_name), ''),
    NULLIF(TRIM(CONCAT_WS(' ', l.first_name, l.last_name)), ''),
    l.first_name,
    'Unknown Lead'
  ) AS display_name,
  COALESCE(NULLIF(TRIM(a.snapshot_event_name), ''), aet.event_type_name, 'Unknown Event Type') AS event_name,
  CAST(a.snapshot_call_category AS text) AS call_category,
  COALESCE(outcome.name, 'No Outcome') AS outcome_name,
  COALESCE(CAST(outcome.role AS text), 'NO_OUTCOME') AS outcome_role,
  a.no_show,
  CAST(a.source AS text) AS appointment_source,
  COALESCE(NULLIF(TRIM(a.host_id), ''), 'No Host') AS host_id,
  COALESCE(NULLIF(TRIM(a.setter_id), ''), 'No Setter') AS setter_id,
  EXISTS (
    SELECT 1
    FROM fathom_call_records f_check
    WHERE f_check.appointment_id = a.id
      AND f_check.clerk_org_id = a.clerk_org_id
  ) AS has_fathom_record
FROM appointments a
LEFT JOIN leads l
  ON l.id = a.lead_id
 AND l.clerk_org_id = a.clerk_org_id
 AND l.is_deleted = false
LEFT JOIN sales_statuses outcome
  ON outcome.id = a.outcome_id
 AND outcome.clerk_org_id = a.clerk_org_id
LEFT JOIN appointment_event_types aet
  ON aet.id = a.appointment_event_type_id
 AND aet.clerk_org_id = a.clerk_org_id
 AND aet.is_deleted = false
WHERE a.clerk_org_id = :org_id
  AND a.is_deleted = false
  AND a.schedule_time < NOW()
  AND a.no_show = false
ORDER BY a.schedule_time DESC, a.id ASC
LIMIT 20;

question: Which past calls were completed and not no-shows?
notes: Past appointment count uses schedule_time < NOW(); attended/completed non-no-show uses no_show = false.
generated_sql:
SELECT
  COUNT(*) OVER() AS total_matching_rows,
  a.id,
  a.schedule_time,
  COALESCE(
    NULLIF(TRIM(l.full_name), ''),
    NULLIF(TRIM(CONCAT_WS(' ', l.first_name, l.last_name)), ''),
    l.first_name,
    'Unknown Lead'
  ) AS display_name,
  COALESCE(NULLIF(TRIM(a.snapshot_event_name), ''), aet.event_type_name, 'Unknown Event Type') AS event_name,
  CAST(a.snapshot_call_category AS text) AS call_category,
  COALESCE(outcome.name, 'No Outcome') AS outcome_name
FROM appointments a
LEFT JOIN leads l
  ON l.id = a.lead_id
 AND l.clerk_org_id = a.clerk_org_id
 AND l.is_deleted = false
LEFT JOIN sales_statuses outcome
  ON outcome.id = a.outcome_id
 AND outcome.clerk_org_id = a.clerk_org_id
LEFT JOIN appointment_event_types aet
  ON aet.id = a.appointment_event_type_id
 AND aet.clerk_org_id = a.clerk_org_id
 AND aet.is_deleted = false
WHERE a.clerk_org_id = :org_id
  AND a.is_deleted = false
  AND a.schedule_time < NOW()
  AND a.no_show = false
ORDER BY a.schedule_time DESC, a.id ASC
LIMIT 20

question: Show call history for the appointment records.
notes: Past appointment count uses schedule_time < NOW(); attended/completed non-no-show uses no_show = false.
generated_sql:
SELECT
  COUNT(*) OVER() AS total_matching_rows,
  a.id AS appointment_id,
  a.schedule_time,
  COALESCE(
    NULLIF(TRIM(l.full_name), ''),
    NULLIF(TRIM(CONCAT_WS(' ', l.first_name, l.last_name)), ''),
    l.first_name,
    'Unknown Lead'
  ) AS display_name,
  COALESCE(NULLIF(TRIM(a.snapshot_event_name), ''), aet.event_type_name, 'Unknown Event Type') AS event_name,
  CAST(a.snapshot_call_category AS text) AS call_category,
  COALESCE(outcome.name, 'No Outcome') AS outcome_name,
  COALESCE(CAST(outcome.role AS text), 'NO_OUTCOME') AS outcome_role,
  a.no_show,
  CAST(a.source AS text) AS appointment_source,
  COALESCE(NULLIF(TRIM(a.host_id), ''), 'No Host') AS host_id,
  COALESCE(NULLIF(TRIM(a.setter_id), ''), 'No Setter') AS setter_id,
  EXISTS (
    SELECT 1
    FROM fathom_call_records f_check
    WHERE f_check.appointment_id = a.id
      AND f_check.clerk_org_id = a.clerk_org_id
  ) AS has_fathom_record
FROM appointments a
LEFT JOIN leads l
  ON l.id = a.lead_id
 AND l.clerk_org_id = a.clerk_org_id
 AND l.is_deleted = false
LEFT JOIN sales_statuses outcome
  ON outcome.id = a.outcome_id
 AND outcome.clerk_org_id = a.clerk_org_id
LEFT JOIN appointment_event_types aet
  ON aet.id = a.appointment_event_type_id
 AND aet.clerk_org_id = a.clerk_org_id
 AND aet.is_deleted = false
WHERE a.clerk_org_id = :org_id
  AND a.is_deleted = false
  AND a.schedule_time < NOW()
ORDER BY a.schedule_time DESC, a.id ASC
LIMIT :limit

question: List the last 20 completed appointments.
notes: Past appointment count uses schedule_time < NOW(); attended/completed non-no-show uses no_show = false.
generated_sql:
SELECT
  COUNT(*) OVER() AS total_matching_rows,
  a.id,
  a.schedule_time,
  COALESCE(
    NULLIF(TRIM(l.full_name), ''),
    NULLIF(TRIM(CONCAT_WS(' ', l.first_name, l.last_name)), ''),
    l.first_name,
    'Unknown Lead'
  ) AS display_name,
  COALESCE(NULLIF(TRIM(a.snapshot_event_name), ''), aet.event_type_name, 'Unknown Event Type') AS event_name,
  CAST(a.snapshot_call_category AS text) AS call_category,
  COALESCE(outcome.name, 'No Outcome') AS outcome_name,
  COALESCE(CAST(outcome.role AS text), 'NO_OUTCOME') AS outcome_role,
  a.no_show,
  CAST(a.source AS text) AS appointment_source,
  COALESCE(NULLIF(TRIM(a.host_id), ''), 'No Host') AS host_id,
  COALESCE(NULLIF(TRIM(a.setter_id), ''), 'No Setter') AS setter_id,
  EXISTS (
    SELECT 1
    FROM fathom_call_records f_check
    WHERE f_check.appointment_id = a.id
      AND f_check.clerk_org_id = a.clerk_org_id
  ) AS has_fathom_record
FROM appointments a
LEFT JOIN leads l
  ON l.id = a.lead_id
 AND l.clerk_org_id = a.clerk_org_id
 AND l.is_deleted = false
LEFT JOIN sales_statuses outcome
  ON outcome.id = a.outcome_id
 AND outcome.clerk_org_id = a.clerk_org_id
LEFT JOIN appointment_event_types aet
  ON aet.id = a.appointment_event_type_id
 AND aet.clerk_org_id = a.clerk_org_id
 AND aet.is_deleted = false
WHERE a.clerk_org_id = :org_id
  AND a.is_deleted = false
  AND a.schedule_time < NOW()
  AND a.no_show = false
ORDER BY a.schedule_time DESC, a.id ASC
LIMIT :limit;

question: Which completed calls have Fathom records?
notes: Past appointment count uses schedule_time < NOW(); attended/completed non-no-show uses no_show = false.
generated_sql:
SELECT
  COUNT(*) OVER() AS total_matching_rows,
  a.id AS appointment_id,
  a.schedule_time,
  COALESCE(
    NULLIF(TRIM(l.full_name), ''),
    NULLIF(TRIM(CONCAT_WS(' ', l.first_name, l.last_name)), ''),
    l.first_name,
    'Unknown Lead'
  ) AS display_name,
  COALESCE(NULLIF(TRIM(a.snapshot_event_name), ''), aet.event_type_name, 'Unknown Event Type') AS event_name,
  CAST(a.snapshot_call_category AS text) AS call_category,
  COALESCE(outcome.name, 'No Outcome') AS outcome_name,
  COALESCE(CAST(outcome.role AS text), 'NO_OUTCOME') AS outcome_role,
  CAST(a.source AS text) AS appointment_source,
  COALESCE(NULLIF(TRIM(a.host_id), ''), 'No Host') AS host_id,
  COALESCE(NULLIF(TRIM(a.setter_id), ''), 'No Setter') AS setter_id
FROM appointments a
LEFT JOIN leads l
  ON l.id = a.lead_id
 AND l.clerk_org_id = a.clerk_org_id
 AND l.is_deleted = false
LEFT JOIN sales_statuses outcome
  ON outcome.id = a.outcome_id
 AND outcome.clerk_org_id = a.clerk_org_id
LEFT JOIN appointment_event_types aet
  ON aet.id = a.appointment_event_type_id
 AND aet.clerk_org_id = a.clerk_org_id
 AND aet.is_deleted = false
WHERE a.clerk_org_id = :org_id
  AND a.is_deleted = false
  AND a.schedule_time < NOW()
  AND a.no_show = false
  AND EXISTS (
    SELECT 1
    FROM fathom_call_records f_check
    WHERE f_check.appointment_id = a.id
      AND f_check.clerk_org_id = a.clerk_org_id
  )
ORDER BY a.schedule_time DESC, a.id ASC
LIMIT 20;

question: Which past appointments do not have Fathom attached?
notes: Past appointment count uses schedule_time < NOW(); attended/completed non-no-show uses no_show = false.
generated_sql:
SELECT
  COUNT(*) OVER() AS total_matching_rows,
  a.id,
  a.schedule_time,
  COALESCE(
    NULLIF(TRIM(l.full_name), ''),
    NULLIF(TRIM(CONCAT_WS(' ', l.first_name, l.last_name)), ''),
    l.first_name,
    'Unknown Lead'
  ) AS display_name,
  COALESCE(NULLIF(TRIM(a.snapshot_event_name), ''), aet.event_type_name, 'Unknown Event Type') AS event_name,
  CAST(a.snapshot_call_category AS text) AS call_category,
  COALESCE(outcome.name, 'No Outcome') AS outcome_name,
  COALESCE(CAST(outcome.role AS text), 'NO_OUTCOME') AS outcome_role,
  a.no_show,
  CAST(a.source AS text) AS appointment_source,
  COALESCE(NULLIF(TRIM(a.host_id), ''), 'No Host') AS host_id,
  COALESCE(NULLIF(TRIM(a.setter_id), ''), 'No Setter') AS setter_id,
  false AS has_fathom_record
FROM appointments a
LEFT JOIN leads l
  ON l.id = a.lead_id
 AND l.clerk_org_id = a.clerk_org_id
 AND l.is_deleted = false
LEFT JOIN sales_statuses outcome
  ON outcome.id = a.outcome_id
 AND outcome.clerk_org_id = a.clerk_org_id
LEFT JOIN appointment_event_types aet
  ON aet.id = a.appointment_event_type_id
 AND aet.clerk_org_id = a.clerk_org_id
 AND aet.is_deleted = false
WHERE a.clerk_org_id = :org_id
  AND a.is_deleted = false
  AND a.schedule_time < NOW()
  AND NOT EXISTS (
    SELECT 1
    FROM fathom_call_records f_check
    WHERE f_check.appointment_id = a.id
      AND f_check.clerk_org_id = a.clerk_org_id
  )
ORDER BY a.schedule_time DESC, a.id ASC
LIMIT 20

question: What is the appointment no-show rate?
notes: No-show rate should use past appointments by default and appointments.no_show as the primary metric.
generated_sql:
SELECT
  COUNT(*) AS total_past_appointments,
  COUNT(*) FILTER (WHERE a.no_show = true) AS no_show_appointments,
  ROUND(
    100.0 * COUNT(*) FILTER (WHERE a.no_show = true) / NULLIF(COUNT(*), 0),
    2
  ) AS no_show_rate_percent
FROM appointments a
WHERE a.clerk_org_id = :org_id
  AND a.is_deleted = false
  AND a.schedule_time < NOW();
generated_final_answer: Out of 449 past appointments, 54 were no-shows. The appointment no-show rate is 12.03%.

question: Which event type has the highest no-show rate?
generated_sql:
WITH event_type_rates AS (
  SELECT
    COALESCE(NULLIF(TRIM(a.snapshot_event_name), ''), aet.event_type_name, 'Unknown Event Type') AS event_name,
    COUNT(*) AS total_past_appointments,
    COUNT(*) FILTER (WHERE a.no_show = true) AS no_show_appointments,
    100.0 * COUNT(*) FILTER (WHERE a.no_show = true) / NULLIF(COUNT(*), 0) AS no_show_rate_percent_raw
  FROM appointments a
  LEFT JOIN appointment_event_types aet
    ON aet.id = a.appointment_event_type_id
   AND aet.clerk_org_id = a.clerk_org_id
   AND aet.is_deleted = false
  WHERE a.clerk_org_id = :org_id
    AND a.is_deleted = false
    AND a.schedule_time < NOW()
  GROUP BY COALESCE(NULLIF(TRIM(a.snapshot_event_name), ''), aet.event_type_name, 'Unknown Event Type')
),
overall AS (
  SELECT
    COALESCE(SUM(total_past_appointments), 0) AS total_matching_past_appointments,
    MAX(no_show_rate_percent_raw) AS max_no_show_rate_percent_raw
  FROM event_type_rates
)
SELECT
  er.event_name,
  er.total_past_appointments,
  er.no_show_appointments,
  ROUND(er.no_show_rate_percent_raw, 2) AS no_show_rate_percent,
  o.total_matching_past_appointments
FROM event_type_rates er
CROSS JOIN overall o
WHERE er.no_show_rate_percent_raw = o.max_no_show_rate_percent_raw
ORDER BY er.total_past_appointments DESC, er.event_name ASC;
generated_final_answer: Across 449 past appointments, the event type labeled “Unknown” has the highest no-show rate at 100.00% — 1 no-show out of 1 past appointment.

question: Which Calendly event is creating the most no-shows?
generated_sql:
WITH calendly_past_appointments AS (
  SELECT
    COALESCE(NULLIF(TRIM(a.snapshot_event_name), ''), aet.event_type_name, 'Unknown Event Type') AS event_name,
    a.no_show
  FROM appointments a
  LEFT JOIN appointment_event_types aet
    ON aet.id = a.appointment_event_type_id
   AND aet.clerk_org_id = a.clerk_org_id
   AND aet.is_deleted = false
  WHERE a.clerk_org_id = :org_id
    AND a.is_deleted = false
    AND a.schedule_time < NOW()
    AND a.source = 'CALENDLY'
),
event_stats AS (
  SELECT
    event_name,
    COUNT(*) AS total_past_appointments,
    COUNT(*) FILTER (WHERE no_show = true) AS no_show_appointments,
    ROUND(
      100.0 * COUNT(*) FILTER (WHERE no_show = true) / NULLIF(COUNT(*), 0),
      2
    ) AS no_show_rate_percent,
    SUM(COUNT(*) FILTER (WHERE no_show = true)) OVER() AS total_matching_no_show_appointments
  FROM calendly_past_appointments
  GROUP BY event_name
),
ranked AS (
  SELECT
    event_name,
    no_show_appointments,
    total_past_appointments,
    no_show_rate_percent,
    total_matching_no_show_appointments,
    ROUND(
      no_show_appointments * 100.0 / NULLIF(total_matching_no_show_appointments, 0),
      2
    ) AS percentage_of_total_no_shows,
    DENSE_RANK() OVER (ORDER BY no_show_appointments DESC, event_name ASC) AS no_show_rank
  FROM event_stats
  WHERE no_show_appointments > 0
)
SELECT
  event_name,
  no_show_appointments,
  total_past_appointments,
  no_show_rate_percent,
  total_matching_no_show_appointments,
  percentage_of_total_no_shows
FROM ranked
WHERE no_show_rank = 1
ORDER BY event_name ASC;

question: Which call category has the worst no-show rate?
generated_sql:
WITH category_rates AS (
  SELECT
    CAST(a.snapshot_call_category AS text) AS call_category,
    COUNT(*) AS total_past_appointments,
    COUNT(*) FILTER (WHERE a.no_show = true) AS no_show_appointments,
    ROUND(
      100.0 * COUNT(*) FILTER (WHERE a.no_show = true) / NULLIF(COUNT(*), 0),
      2
    ) AS no_show_rate_percent,
    SUM(COUNT(*)) OVER() AS total_matching_past_appointments
  FROM appointments a
  WHERE a.clerk_org_id = :org_id
    AND a.is_deleted = false
    AND a.schedule_time < NOW()
    AND a.snapshot_call_category IS NOT NULL
  GROUP BY CAST(a.snapshot_call_category AS text)
),
max_rate AS (
  SELECT MAX(no_show_rate_percent) AS max_no_show_rate_percent
  FROM category_rates
)
SELECT
  cr.call_category,
  cr.total_past_appointments,
  cr.no_show_appointments,
  cr.no_show_rate_percent,
  cr.total_matching_past_appointments
FROM category_rates cr
JOIN max_rate mr
  ON cr.no_show_rate_percent = mr.max_no_show_rate_percent
ORDER BY cr.total_past_appointments DESC, cr.call_category ASC;

WITH category_counts AS (
  SELECT
    COALESCE(CAST(a.snapshot_call_category AS text), 'UNKNOWN') AS call_category,
    COUNT(*) AS total_past_appointments,
    COUNT(*) FILTER (WHERE a.no_show = true) AS no_show_appointments
  FROM appointments a

    AND a.is_deleted = false

  GROUP BY COALESCE(CAST(a.snapshot_call_category AS text), 'UNKNOWN')
),
totals AS (
  SELECT
    SUM(total_past_appointments) AS total_matching_past_appointments,
    SUM(no_show_appointments) AS total_matching_no_show_appointments
  FROM category_counts
)
SELECT

    100.0 * cc.no_show_appointments / NULLIF(cc.total_past_appointments, 0),

WITH sources AS (

  UNION ALL
  SELECT 'MANUAL'::text AS appointment_source

source_stats AS (

  FROM appointments a
  WHERE a.clerk_org_id = :org_id
    AND a.is_deleted = false
    AND a.schedule_time < NOW()
    AND a.source IN ('CALENDLY', 'MANUAL')
  GROUP BY CAST(a.source AS text)
)
SELECT
  s.appointment_source,
  COALESCE(ss.total_past_appointments, 0) AS total_past_appointments,
  COALESCE(ss.no_show_appointments, 0) AS no_show_appointments,
  CASE
    WHEN COALESCE(ss.total_past_appointments, 0) = 0 THEN NULL
    ELSE ROUND(
      100.0 * COALESCE(ss.no_show_appointments, 0) / NULLIF(COALESCE(ss.total_past_appointments, 0), 0),
      2
    )
  END AS no_show_rate_percent,
  SUM(COALESCE(ss.no_show_appointments, 0)) OVER() AS total_matching_no_show_appointments,
  CASE
    WHEN SUM(COALESCE(ss.no_show_appointments, 0)) OVER() = 0 THEN NULL
    ELSE ROUND(
      100.0 * COALESCE(ss.no_show_appointments, 0) / NULLIF(SUM(COALESCE(ss.no_show_appointments, 0)) OVER(), 0),
      2
    )
  END AS percentage_of_total_no_shows
FROM sources s
LEFT JOIN source_stats ss
  ON ss.appointment_source = s.appointment_source
ORDER BY no_show_appointments DESC, s.appointment_source ASC

  FROM filtered_appointments
  GROUP BY event_name
),
overall_totals AS (

WITH event_type_counts AS (
  SELECT
    COALESCE(NULLIF(TRIM(a.snapshot_event_name), ''), aet.event_type_name, 'Unknown Event Type') AS event_name,
    COUNT(*) AS missed_call_count,
    SUM(COUNT(*)) OVER() AS total_matching_missed_calls,
    ROUND(
      COUNT(*) * 100.0 / NULLIF(SUM(COUNT(*)) OVER(), 0),
      2
    ) AS percentage_of_total_missed_calls
  FROM appointments a
  LEFT JOIN appointment_event_types aet
    ON aet.id = a.appointment_event_type_id
   AND aet.clerk_org_id = a.clerk_org_id
   AND aet.is_deleted = false
  WHERE a.clerk_org_id = :org_id
    AND a.is_deleted = false
    AND a.no_show = true
    AND a.schedule_time >= DATE_TRUNC('week', NOW()) - INTERVAL '1 week'
    AND a.schedule_time < DATE_TRUNC('week', NOW())
  GROUP BY COALESCE(NULLIF(TRIM(a.snapshot_event_name), ''), aet.event_type_name, 'Unknown Event Type')
),
max_count AS (
  SELECT MAX(missed_call_count) AS max_missed_call_count
  FROM event_type_counts
)
SELECT
  etc.event_name,
  etc.missed_call_count,
  etc.percentage_of_total_missed_calls,
  etc.total_matching_missed_calls
FROM event_type_counts etc
JOIN max_count mc
  ON etc.missed_call_count = mc.max_missed_call_count

question: Which call category has the highest missed appointment rate?
generated_sql:
WITH category_rates AS (
  SELECT
    COALESCE(CAST(a.snapshot_call_category AS text), 'UNKNOWN_CALL_CATEGORY') AS call_category,
    COUNT(*) AS total_past_appointments,
    COUNT(*) FILTER (WHERE a.no_show = true) AS missed_appointments,
    ROUND(
      100.0 * COUNT(*) FILTER (WHERE a.no_show = true) / NULLIF(COUNT(*), 0),
      2
    ) AS missed_appointment_rate_percent,
    ((COUNT(*) FILTER (WHERE a.no_show = true))::numeric / NULLIF(COUNT(*), 0)) AS missed_appointment_rate_ratio,
    SUM(COUNT(*)) OVER () AS total_matching_past_appointments
  FROM appointments a
  WHERE a.clerk_org_id = :org_id
    AND a.is_deleted = false
    AND a.schedule_time < NOW()
  GROUP BY COALESCE(CAST(a.snapshot_call_category AS text), 'UNKNOWN_CALL_CATEGORY')
),
ranked_categories AS (
  SELECT
    call_category,
    total_past_appointments,
    missed_appointments,
    missed_appointment_rate_percent,
    total_matching_past_appointments,
    RANK() OVER (ORDER BY missed_appointment_rate_ratio DESC NULLS LAST) AS missed_rate_rank
  FROM category_rates
)
SELECT
  call_category,
  total_past_appointments,
  missed_appointments,
  missed_appointment_rate_percent,
  total_matching_past_appointments
FROM ranked_categories
WHERE missed_rate_rank = 1
ORDER BY total_past_appointments DESC, call_category ASC;

question: Which type of appointment is causing the most no-shows?

WITH event_type_stats AS (
  SELECT
    COALESCE(NULLIF(TRIM(a.snapshot_event_name), ''), aet.event_type_name, 'Unknown Event Type') AS event_name,
    COUNT(*) AS total_past_appointments,
    COUNT(*) FILTER (WHERE a.no_show = true) AS no_show_appointments,
    ROUND(
      100.0 * COUNT(*) FILTER (WHERE a.no_show = true) / NULLIF(COUNT(*), 0),
      2
    ) AS no_show_rate_percent
  FROM appointments a
  LEFT JOIN appointment_event_types aet
    ON aet.id = a.appointment_event_type_id
   AND aet.clerk_org_id = a.clerk_org_id
   AND aet.is_deleted = false
  WHERE a.clerk_org_id = :org_id
    AND a.is_deleted = false
    AND a.schedule_time < NOW()
  GROUP BY COALESCE(NULLIF(TRIM(a.snapshot_event_name), ''), aet.event_type_name, 'Unknown Event Type')
),
no_show_event_types AS (
  SELECT
    event_name,
    total_past_appointments,
    no_show_appointments,
    no_show_rate_percent
  FROM event_type_stats
  WHERE no_show_appointments > 0
),
max_no_shows AS (
  SELECT MAX(no_show_appointments) AS max_no_show_appointments
  FROM no_show_event_types
),
no_show_totals AS (
  SELECT SUM(no_show_appointments) AS total_matching_no_show_appointments
  FROM no_show_event_types
)
SELECT
  n.event_name,
  n.no_show_appointments,
  n.total_past_appointments,
  n.no_show_rate_percent,
  t.total_matching_no_show_appointments,
  ROUND(
    n.no_show_appointments * 100.0 / NULLIF(t.total_matching_no_show_appointments, 0),
    2
  ) AS percentage_of_total_no_shows
FROM no_show_event_types n
JOIN max_no_shows m
  ON n.no_show_appointments = m.max_no_show_appointments
CROSS JOIN no_show_totals t
ORDER BY n.event_name ASC

WITH outcome_counts AS (

    COALESCE(outcome.name, 'No Outcome') AS outcome_name,

    COUNT(*) AS appointment_count
  FROM appointments a

    ON outcome.id = a.outcome_id
   AND outcome.clerk_org_id = a.clerk_org_id
  WHERE a.clerk_org_id = :org_id
    AND a.is_deleted = false
  GROUP BY
    COALESCE(outcome.name, 'No Outcome'),
    COALESCE(CAST(outcome.role AS text), 'NO_OUTCOME')
),
ranked_outcomes AS (
  SELECT
    outcome_name,
    outcome_role,
    appointment_count,
    SUM(appointment_count) OVER() AS total_matching_appointments,
    ROUND(
      appointment_count * 100.0 / NULLIF(SUM(appointment_count) OVER(), 0),

    ) AS percentage_of_total,

)

question: What percentage of calls ended as won, lost, follow-up, or no-show?
generated_sql:
WITH categories(outcome_role) AS (
  VALUES
    ('WON'),
    ('LOST'),
    ('FOLLOW_UP'),
    ('NO_SHOW')
), categorized_calls AS (
  SELECT
    CASE
      WHEN a.no_show = true THEN 'NO_SHOW'
      WHEN CAST(outcome.role AS text) IN ('WON', 'LOST', 'FOLLOW_UP') THEN CAST(outcome.role AS text)
      WHEN CAST(outcome.role AS text) = 'NO_SHOW' THEN 'NO_SHOW'
      ELSE NULL
    END AS outcome_role
  FROM appointments a
  LEFT JOIN sales_statuses outcome
    ON outcome.id = a.outcome_id
   AND outcome.clerk_org_id = a.clerk_org_id
  WHERE a.clerk_org_id = :org_id
    AND a.is_deleted = false
    AND a.schedule_time < NOW()
), counts AS (
  SELECT
    c.outcome_role,
    COUNT(cc.outcome_role) AS call_count
  FROM categories c
  LEFT JOIN categorized_calls cc
    ON cc.outcome_role = c.outcome_role
  GROUP BY c.outcome_role
)
SELECT
  outcome_role,

question: Which appointment outcome is used the most?
generated_sql:
WITH outcome_counts AS (
  SELECT
    outcome.name AS outcome_name,
    CAST(outcome.role AS text) AS outcome_role,
    COUNT(*) AS appointment_count
  FROM appointments a
  JOIN sales_statuses outcome
    ON outcome.id = a.outcome_id
   AND outcome.clerk_org_id = a.clerk_org_id
  WHERE a.clerk_org_id = :org_id
    AND a.is_deleted = false
  GROUP BY outcome.name, CAST(outcome.role AS text)
),
max_count AS (
  SELECT MAX(appointment_count) AS max_appointment_count
  FROM outcome_counts
),
totals AS (
  SELECT SUM(appointment_count) AS total_matching_appointments
  FROM outcome_counts
)
SELECT
  oc.outcome_name,
  oc.outcome_role,
  oc.appointment_count,
  t.total_matching_appointments,
  ROUND(oc.appointment_count * 100.0 / NULLIF(t.total_matching_appointments, 0), 2) AS percentage_of_total
FROM outcome_counts oc
JOIN max_count mc
  ON oc.appointment_count = mc.max_appointment_count
CROSS JOIN totals t
ORDER BY oc.outcome_name ASC;

    COUNT(*) AS appointment_count
  FROM appointments a
  WHERE a.clerk_org_id = :org_id
    AND a.is_deleted = false
    AND CAST(a.source AS text) IN ('CALENDLY', 'MANUAL')
  GROUP BY CAST(a.source AS text)

SELECT
  sl.appointment_source,
  COALESCE(sc.appointment_count, 0) AS appointment_count,
  SUM(COALESCE(sc.appointment_count, 0)) OVER() AS total_matching_appointments,

    COALESCE(sc.appointment_count, 0) * 100.0

FROM source_labels sl
LEFT JOIN source_counts sc
  ON sc.appointment_source = sl.appointment_source
ORDER BY CASE sl.appointment_source
  WHEN 'CALENDLY' THEN 1
  WHEN 'MANUAL' THEN 2

question: Which event type has the fewest appointments?
generated_sql:
WITH event_type_counts AS (
  SELECT
    COALESCE(NULLIF(TRIM(a.snapshot_event_name), ''), aet.event_type_name, 'Unknown Event Type') AS event_name,
    COUNT(*) AS appointment_count
  FROM appointments a
  LEFT JOIN appointment_event_types aet
    ON aet.id = a.appointment_event_type_id
   AND aet.clerk_org_id = a.clerk_org_id
   AND aet.is_deleted = false
  WHERE a.clerk_org_id = :org_id
    AND a.is_deleted = false
  GROUP BY COALESCE(NULLIF(TRIM(a.snapshot_event_name), ''), aet.event_type_name, 'Unknown Event Type')
), totals AS (
  SELECT
    SUM(appointment_count) AS total_matching_appointments,
    MIN(appointment_count) AS min_appointment_count
  FROM event_type_counts
)
SELECT
  etc.event_name,
  etc.appointment_count,
  t.total_matching_appointments,
  ROUND(etc.appointment_count * 100.0 / NULLIF(t.total_matching_appointments, 0), 2) AS percentage_of_total
FROM event_type_counts etc
CROSS JOIN totals t
WHERE etc.appointment_count = t.min_appointment_count
ORDER BY etc.event_name ASC;

question: Which setter booked the most sales calls?
generated_sql:
WITH setter_counts AS (
  SELECT
    NULLIF(TRIM(a.setter_id), '') AS setter_id,
    COUNT(*) AS sales_call_count
  FROM appointments a
  WHERE a.clerk_org_id = :org_id
    AND a.is_deleted = false
    AND a.snapshot_call_category = 'SALES_CALL'
    AND NULLIF(TRIM(a.setter_id), '') IS NOT NULL
  GROUP BY NULLIF(TRIM(a.setter_id), '')
), totals AS (
  SELECT SUM(sales_call_count) AS total_matching_appointments
  FROM setter_counts
), max_count AS (
  SELECT MAX(sales_call_count) AS max_sales_call_count
  FROM setter_counts
)
SELECT
  sc.setter_id,
  sc.sales_call_count,
  t.total_matching_appointments,
  ROUND(sc.sales_call_count * 100.0 / NULLIF(t.total_matching_appointments, 0), 2) AS percentage_of_total
FROM setter_counts sc
CROSS JOIN totals t
JOIN max_count mc
  ON sc.sales_call_count = mc.max_sales_call_count
ORDER BY sc.setter_id ASC;
generated_final_answer: The top setter ID is `user_3AesMObeGeZynVoGhloMkfwanES`, with 92 booked sales calls out of 93 sales calls that had a setter recorded (98.92%).

question: Which setter booked the most calls this month?
generated_sql:
WITH setter_counts AS (
  SELECT
    NULLIF(TRIM(a.setter_id), '') AS setter_id,
    COUNT(*) AS appointment_count
  FROM appointments a
  WHERE a.clerk_org_id = :org_id
    AND a.is_deleted = false
    AND NULLIF(TRIM(a.setter_id), '') IS NOT NULL
    AND a.schedule_time >= DATE_TRUNC('month', CURRENT_DATE)
    AND a.schedule_time < DATE_TRUNC('month', CURRENT_DATE) + INTERVAL '1 month'
  GROUP BY NULLIF(TRIM(a.setter_id), '')
),
summary AS (
  SELECT
    MAX(appointment_count) AS max_appointment_count,
    SUM(appointment_count) AS total_matching_appointments
  FROM setter_counts
)
SELECT
  sc.setter_id,
  sc.appointment_count,
  s.total_matching_appointments,
  ROUND(sc.appointment_count * 100.0 / NULLIF(s.total_matching_appointments, 0), 2) AS percentage_of_total
FROM setter_counts sc
CROSS JOIN summary s
WHERE sc.appointment_count = s.max_appointment_count
ORDER BY sc.setter_id ASC;
generated_final_answer: This month, setter `user_3AesMObeGeZynVoGhloMkfwanES` booked the most calls, with 1 scheduled call.

question: Which setter has the most attended calls?

    COUNT(*) AS attended_call_count

    AND a.is_deleted = false
    AND a.schedule_time < NOW()
    AND a.no_show = false
    AND NULLIF(TRIM(a.setter_id), '') IS NOT NULL

)

  attended_call_count,

  percentage_of_total

ORDER BY setter_id ASC;

    COUNT(*) AS appointment_count
  FROM appointments a
  WHERE a.clerk_org_id = :org_id

  GROUP BY

)
SELECT

  ROUND(

  ) AS percentage_of_setter

question: Show booked call volume by setter for last week.
generated_sql:
SELECT
  COALESCE(NULLIF(TRIM(a.setter_id), ''), 'No Setter') AS setter_id,
  COUNT(*) AS appointment_count,
  SUM(COUNT(*)) OVER() AS total_matching_appointments,
  ROUND(COUNT(*) * 100.0 / NULLIF(SUM(COUNT(*)) OVER(), 0), 2) AS percentage_of_total
FROM appointments a
WHERE a.clerk_org_id = :org_id

  AND a.schedule_time >= :start_date
  AND a.schedule_time < :end_date

question: Show me all appointments.
generated_sql:
SELECT
  COUNT(*) OVER() AS total_matching_rows,
  a.id AS appointment_id,
  a.schedule_time,
  COALESCE(
    NULLIF(TRIM(l.full_name), ''),
    NULLIF(TRIM(CONCAT_WS(' ', l.first_name, l.last_name)), ''),
    l.first_name,
    'Unknown Lead'
  ) AS display_name,
  COALESCE(NULLIF(TRIM(a.snapshot_event_name), ''), aet.event_type_name, 'Unknown Event Type') AS event_name,
  CAST(a.snapshot_call_category AS text) AS call_category,
  COALESCE(outcome.name, 'No Outcome') AS outcome_name,
  COALESCE(CAST(outcome.role AS text), 'NO_OUTCOME') AS outcome_role,
  a.no_show,
  CAST(a.source AS text) AS appointment_source,
  COALESCE(NULLIF(TRIM(a.host_id), ''), 'No Host') AS host_id,
  COALESCE(NULLIF(TRIM(a.setter_id), ''), 'No Setter') AS setter_id,
  EXISTS (
    SELECT 1
    FROM fathom_call_records f_check
    WHERE f_check.appointment_id = a.id
      AND f_check.clerk_org_id = a.clerk_org_id
  ) AS has_fathom_record
FROM appointments a
LEFT JOIN leads l
  ON l.id = a.lead_id
 AND l.clerk_org_id = a.clerk_org_id
 AND l.is_deleted = false
LEFT JOIN sales_statuses outcome
  ON outcome.id = a.outcome_id
 AND outcome.clerk_org_id = a.clerk_org_id
LEFT JOIN appointment_event_types aet
  ON aet.id = a.appointment_event_type_id
 AND aet.clerk_org_id = a.clerk_org_id
 AND aet.is_deleted = false
WHERE a.clerk_org_id = :org_id
  AND a.is_deleted = false
ORDER BY a.schedule_time DESC, a.id ASC
LIMIT :limit

question: Which appointments do we have in the system?
generated_sql:
SELECT
  COUNT(*) OVER() AS total_matching_rows,
  a.id,
  a.schedule_time,
  COALESCE(
    NULLIF(TRIM(l.full_name), ''),
    NULLIF(TRIM(CONCAT_WS(' ', l.first_name, l.last_name)), ''),
    l.first_name,
    'Unknown Lead'
  ) AS display_name,
  COALESCE(NULLIF(TRIM(a.snapshot_event_name), ''), aet.event_type_name, 'Unknown Event Type') AS event_name,
  CAST(a.snapshot_call_category AS text) AS call_category,
  COALESCE(outcome.name, 'No Outcome') AS outcome_name,
  COALESCE(CAST(outcome.role AS text), 'NO_OUTCOME') AS outcome_role,
  a.no_show,
  CAST(a.source AS text) AS appointment_source,
  COALESCE(NULLIF(TRIM(a.host_id), ''), 'No Host') AS host_id,
  COALESCE(NULLIF(TRIM(a.setter_id), ''), 'No Setter') AS setter_id,
  EXISTS (
    SELECT 1
    FROM fathom_call_records f_check
    WHERE f_check.appointment_id = a.id
      AND f_check.clerk_org_id = a.clerk_org_id
  ) AS has_fathom_record
FROM appointments a
LEFT JOIN leads l
  ON l.id = a.lead_id
 AND l.clerk_org_id = a.clerk_org_id
 AND l.is_deleted = false
LEFT JOIN sales_statuses outcome
  ON outcome.id = a.outcome_id
 AND outcome.clerk_org_id = a.clerk_org_id
LEFT JOIN appointment_event_types aet
  ON aet.id = a.appointment_event_type_id
 AND aet.clerk_org_id = a.clerk_org_id
 AND aet.is_deleted = false
WHERE a.clerk_org_id = :org_id
  AND a.is_deleted = false
ORDER BY a.schedule_time DESC, a.id ASC
LIMIT :limit;

    NULLIF(TRIM(CONCAT_WS(' ', l.first_name, l.last_name)), ''),
    l.first_name,
    'Unknown Lead'
  ) AS display_name,
  COALESCE(NULLIF(TRIM(a.snapshot_event_name), ''), aet.event_type_name, 'Unknown Event Type') AS event_name,
  CAST(a.snapshot_call_category AS text) AS call_category,
  COALESCE(outcome.name, 'No Outcome') AS outcome_name,
  COALESCE(CAST(outcome.role AS text), 'NO_OUTCOME') AS outcome_role,
  a.no_show,
  CAST(a.source AS text) AS appointment_source,
  COALESCE(NULLIF(TRIM(a.host_id), ''), 'No Host') AS host_id,
  COALESCE(NULLIF(TRIM(a.setter_id), ''), 'No Setter') AS setter_id,
  EXISTS (
    SELECT 1
    FROM fathom_call_records f_check
    WHERE f_check.appointment_id = a.id
      AND f_check.clerk_org_id = a.clerk_org_id
  ) AS has_fathom_record
FROM appointments a
LEFT JOIN leads l
  ON l.id = a.lead_id
 AND l.clerk_org_id = a.clerk_org_id
 AND l.is_deleted = false
LEFT JOIN sales_statuses outcome
  ON outcome.id = a.outcome_id
 AND outcome.clerk_org_id = a.clerk_org_id
LEFT JOIN appointment_event_types aet
  ON aet.id = a.appointment_event_type_id
 AND aet.clerk_org_id = a.clerk_org_id
 AND aet.is_deleted = false
WHERE a.clerk_org_id = :org_id
  AND a.is_deleted = false
ORDER BY a.schedule_time DESC, a.id ASC
LIMIT 20;

question: Show me calls with outcome and host.
generated_sql:
SELECT
  COUNT(*) OVER() AS total_matching_rows,
  a.id AS call_id,
  a.schedule_time,
  COALESCE(
    NULLIF(TRIM(l.full_name), ''),
    NULLIF(TRIM(CONCAT_WS(' ', l.first_name, l.last_name)), ''),
    l.first_name,
    'Unknown Lead'
  ) AS display_name,
  COALESCE(NULLIF(TRIM(a.snapshot_event_name), ''), aet.event_type_name, 'Unknown Event Type') AS event_name,
  CASE
    WHEN a.outcome_id IS NULL THEN 'No Outcome'
    ELSE COALESCE(
      NULLIF(TRIM(outcome.name), ''),
      INITCAP(REPLACE(CAST(outcome.role AS text), '_', ' ')),
      'Unknown Outcome'
    )
  END AS outcome_name,
  COALESCE(NULLIF(TRIM(a.host_id), ''), 'No Host') AS host_id
FROM appointments a
LEFT JOIN leads l
  ON l.id = a.lead_id
 AND l.clerk_org_id = a.clerk_org_id
 AND l.is_deleted = false
LEFT JOIN sales_statuses outcome
  ON outcome.id = a.outcome_id
 AND outcome.clerk_org_id = a.clerk_org_id
LEFT JOIN appointment_event_types aet
  ON aet.id = a.appointment_event_type_id
 AND aet.clerk_org_id = a.clerk_org_id
 AND aet.is_deleted = false
WHERE a.clerk_org_id = :org_id
  AND a.is_deleted = false
ORDER BY a.schedule_time DESC, a.id ASC
LIMIT 20

question: List appointments scheduled this month.
generated_sql:
SELECT
  COUNT(*) OVER() AS total_matching_rows,
  a.id,
  a.schedule_time,
  COALESCE(
    NULLIF(TRIM(l.full_name), ''),
    NULLIF(TRIM(CONCAT_WS(' ', l.first_name, l.last_name)), ''),
    l.first_name,
    'Unknown Lead'
  ) AS display_name,
  COALESCE(NULLIF(TRIM(a.snapshot_event_name), ''), aet.event_type_name, 'Unknown Event Type') AS event_name,
  CAST(a.snapshot_call_category AS text) AS call_category,
  COALESCE(outcome.name, 'No Outcome') AS outcome_name,
  COALESCE(CAST(outcome.role AS text), 'NO_OUTCOME') AS outcome_role,
  a.no_show,
  CAST(a.source AS text) AS appointment_source,
  COALESCE(NULLIF(TRIM(a.host_id), ''), 'No Host') AS host_id,
  COALESCE(NULLIF(TRIM(a.setter_id), ''), 'No Setter') AS setter_id,
  EXISTS (
    SELECT 1
    FROM fathom_call_records f_check
    WHERE f_check.appointment_id = a.id
      AND f_check.clerk_org_id = a.clerk_org_id
  ) AS has_fathom_record
FROM appointments a
LEFT JOIN leads l
  ON l.id = a.lead_id
 AND l.clerk_org_id = a.clerk_org_id
 AND l.is_deleted = false
LEFT JOIN sales_statuses outcome
  ON outcome.id = a.outcome_id
 AND outcome.clerk_org_id = a.clerk_org_id
LEFT JOIN appointment_event_types aet
  ON aet.id = a.appointment_event_type_id
 AND aet.clerk_org_id = a.clerk_org_id
 AND aet.is_deleted = false
WHERE a.clerk_org_id = :org_id
  AND a.is_deleted = false
  AND a.schedule_time >= :start_date
  AND a.schedule_time < :end_date
ORDER BY a.schedule_time ASC, a.id ASC
LIMIT :limit

  COALESCE(NULLIF(TRIM(a.snapshot_event_name), ''), aet.event_type_name, 'Unknown Event Type') AS event_name,
  CAST(a.snapshot_call_category AS text) AS call_category,
  COALESCE(outcome.name, 'No Outcome') AS outcome_name,
  COALESCE(CAST(outcome.role AS text), 'NO_OUTCOME') AS outcome_role,
  a.no_show,
  CAST(a.source AS text) AS appointment_source,
  COALESCE(NULLIF(TRIM(a.host_id), ''), 'No Host') AS host_id,
  COALESCE(NULLIF(TRIM(a.setter_id), ''), 'No Setter') AS setter_id,
  EXISTS (
    SELECT 1
    FROM fathom_call_records f_check
    WHERE f_check.appointment_id = a.id
      AND f_check.clerk_org_id = a.clerk_org_id
  ) AS has_fathom_record
FROM appointments a
LEFT JOIN leads l
  ON l.id = a.lead_id
 AND l.clerk_org_id = a.clerk_org_id
 AND l.is_deleted = false
LEFT JOIN sales_statuses outcome
  ON outcome.id = a.outcome_id
 AND outcome.clerk_org_id = a.clerk_org_id
LEFT JOIN appointment_event_types aet
  ON aet.id = a.appointment_event_type_id
 AND aet.clerk_org_id = a.clerk_org_id
 AND aet.is_deleted = false
WHERE a.clerk_org_id = :org_id
  AND a.is_deleted = false
ORDER BY a.schedule_time DESC, a.id ASC
LIMIT :limit;

  COALESCE(NULLIF(TRIM(a.setter_id), ''), 'No Setter') AS setter_id,
  EXISTS (
    SELECT 1
    FROM fathom_call_records f_check
    WHERE f_check.appointment_id = a.id
      AND f_check.clerk_org_id = a.clerk_org_id
  ) AS has_fathom_record
FROM appointments a
LEFT JOIN leads l
  ON l.id = a.lead_id
 AND l.clerk_org_id = a.clerk_org_id

LEFT JOIN sales_statuses outcome
  ON outcome.id = a.outcome_id
 AND outcome.clerk_org_id = a.clerk_org_id
LEFT JOIN appointment_event_types aet
  ON aet.id = a.appointment_event_type_id
 AND aet.clerk_org_id = a.clerk_org_id
 AND aet.is_deleted = false
WHERE a.clerk_org_id = :org_id
  AND a.is_deleted = false
ORDER BY a.schedule_time DESC, a.id ASC

question: List sales call appointments only.
generated_sql:
SELECT
  COUNT(*) OVER() AS total_matching_rows,
  a.id,
  a.schedule_time,
  COALESCE(
    NULLIF(TRIM(l.full_name), ''),
    NULLIF(TRIM(CONCAT_WS(' ', l.first_name, l.last_name)), ''),
    l.first_name,
    'Unknown Lead'
  ) AS display_name,
  COALESCE(NULLIF(TRIM(a.snapshot_event_name), ''), aet.event_type_name, 'Unknown Event Type') AS event_name,
  CAST(a.snapshot_call_category AS text) AS call_category,
  COALESCE(outcome.name, 'No Outcome') AS outcome_name,
  COALESCE(CAST(outcome.role AS text), 'NO_OUTCOME') AS outcome_role,
  a.no_show,
  CAST(a.source AS text) AS appointment_source,
  COALESCE(NULLIF(TRIM(a.host_id), ''), 'No Host') AS host_id,
  COALESCE(NULLIF(TRIM(a.setter_id), ''), 'No Setter') AS setter_id,
  EXISTS (
    SELECT 1
    FROM fathom_call_records f_check
    WHERE f_check.appointment_id = a.id
      AND f_check.clerk_org_id = a.clerk_org_id
  ) AS has_fathom_record
FROM appointments a
LEFT JOIN leads l
  ON l.id = a.lead_id
 AND l.clerk_org_id = a.clerk_org_id
 AND l.is_deleted = false
LEFT JOIN sales_statuses outcome
  ON outcome.id = a.outcome_id
 AND outcome.clerk_org_id = a.clerk_org_id
LEFT JOIN appointment_event_types aet
  ON aet.id = a.appointment_event_type_id
 AND aet.clerk_org_id = a.clerk_org_id
 AND aet.is_deleted = false
WHERE a.clerk_org_id = :org_id
  AND a.is_deleted = false
  AND CAST(a.snapshot_call_category AS text) = 'SALES_CALL'
ORDER BY a.schedule_time DESC, a.id ASC
LIMIT 20

question: Show coaching call appointments only.
generated_sql:
SELECT
  COUNT(*) OVER() AS total_matching_rows,
  a.id,
  a.schedule_time,
  COALESCE(
    NULLIF(TRIM(l.full_name), ''),
    NULLIF(TRIM(CONCAT_WS(' ', l.first_name, l.last_name)), ''),
    l.first_name,
    'Unknown Lead'
  ) AS display_name,
  COALESCE(NULLIF(TRIM(a.snapshot_event_name), ''), aet.event_type_name, 'Unknown Event Type') AS event_name,
  CAST(a.snapshot_call_category AS text) AS call_category,
  COALESCE(outcome.name, 'No Outcome') AS outcome_name,
  COALESCE(CAST(outcome.role AS text), 'NO_OUTCOME') AS outcome_role,
  a.no_show,
  CAST(a.source AS text) AS appointment_source,
  COALESCE(NULLIF(TRIM(a.host_id), ''), 'No Host') AS host_id,
  COALESCE(NULLIF(TRIM(a.setter_id), ''), 'No Setter') AS setter_id,
  EXISTS (
    SELECT 1
    FROM fathom_call_records f_check
    WHERE f_check.appointment_id = a.id
      AND f_check.clerk_org_id = a.clerk_org_id
  ) AS has_fathom_record
FROM appointments a
LEFT JOIN leads l
  ON l.id = a.lead_id
 AND l.clerk_org_id = a.clerk_org_id
 AND l.is_deleted = false
LEFT JOIN sales_statuses outcome
  ON outcome.id = a.outcome_id
 AND outcome.clerk_org_id = a.clerk_org_id
LEFT JOIN appointment_event_types aet
  ON aet.id = a.appointment_event_type_id
 AND aet.clerk_org_id = a.clerk_org_id
 AND aet.is_deleted = false
WHERE a.clerk_org_id = :org_id
  AND a.is_deleted = false
  AND a.snapshot_call_category = 'COACHING_CALL'
ORDER BY a.schedule_time DESC, a.id ASC
LIMIT 20;
generated_final_answer: No coaching call appointments were found.

question: Which appointments ended as Lost?
generated_sql:
SELECT
  COUNT(*) OVER() AS total_matching_rows,
  a.id,
  a.schedule_time,
  COALESCE(
    NULLIF(TRIM(l.full_name), ''),
    NULLIF(TRIM(CONCAT_WS(' ', l.first_name, l.last_name)), ''),
    l.first_name,
    'Unknown Lead'
  ) AS display_name,
  COALESCE(NULLIF(TRIM(a.snapshot_event_name), ''), aet.event_type_name, 'Unknown Event Type') AS event_name,
  CAST(a.snapshot_call_category AS text) AS call_category,
  COALESCE(outcome.name, 'No Outcome') AS outcome_name,
  COALESCE(CAST(outcome.role AS text), 'NO_OUTCOME') AS outcome_role,
  a.no_show,
  CAST(a.source AS text) AS appointment_source,
  COALESCE(NULLIF(TRIM(a.host_id), ''), 'No Host') AS host_id,
  COALESCE(NULLIF(TRIM(a.setter_id), ''), 'No Setter') AS setter_id
FROM appointments a
LEFT JOIN leads l
  ON l.id = a.lead_id
 AND l.clerk_org_id = a.clerk_org_id
 AND l.is_deleted = false
LEFT JOIN sales_statuses outcome
  ON outcome.id = a.outcome_id
 AND outcome.clerk_org_id = a.clerk_org_id
LEFT JOIN appointment_event_types aet
  ON aet.id = a.appointment_event_type_id
 AND aet.clerk_org_id = a.clerk_org_id
 AND aet.is_deleted = false
WHERE a.clerk_org_id = :org_id
  AND a.is_deleted = false
  AND outcome.role = 'LOST'
ORDER BY a.schedule_time DESC, a.id ASC
LIMIT 20

question: Show appointments that have no outcome label.
generated_sql:
SELECT
  COUNT(*) OVER() AS total_matching_rows,
  a.id,
  a.schedule_time,
  COALESCE(
    NULLIF(TRIM(l.full_name), ''),
    NULLIF(TRIM(CONCAT_WS(' ', l.first_name, l.last_name)), ''),
    l.first_name,
    'Unknown Lead'
  ) AS display_name,
  COALESCE(NULLIF(TRIM(a.snapshot_event_name), ''), aet.event_type_name, 'Unknown Event Type') AS event_name,
  CAST(a.snapshot_call_category AS text) AS call_category,
  COALESCE(NULLIF(TRIM(outcome.name), ''), 'No Outcome') AS outcome_name,
  COALESCE(CAST(outcome.role AS text), 'NO_OUTCOME') AS outcome_role,
  a.no_show,
  CAST(a.source AS text) AS appointment_source,
  COALESCE(NULLIF(TRIM(a.host_id), ''), 'No Host') AS host_id,
  COALESCE(NULLIF(TRIM(a.setter_id), ''), 'No Setter') AS setter_id,
  EXISTS (
    SELECT 1
    FROM fathom_call_records f_check
    WHERE f_check.appointment_id = a.id
      AND f_check.clerk_org_id = a.clerk_org_id
  ) AS has_fathom_record
FROM appointments a
LEFT JOIN leads l
  ON l.id = a.lead_id
 AND l.clerk_org_id = a.clerk_org_id
 AND l.is_deleted = false
LEFT JOIN sales_statuses outcome
  ON outcome.id = a.outcome_id
 AND outcome.clerk_org_id = a.clerk_org_id
LEFT JOIN appointment_event_types aet
  ON aet.id = a.appointment_event_type_id
 AND aet.clerk_org_id = a.clerk_org_id
 AND aet.is_deleted = false
WHERE a.clerk_org_id = :org_id
  AND a.is_deleted = false
  AND NULLIF(TRIM(outcome.name), '') IS NULL
ORDER BY a.schedule_time DESC, a.id ASC
LIMIT 20;
generated_final_answer: There are no appointments without an outcome label.

question: List appointments with their Fathom status.
generated_sql:
SELECT
  COUNT(*) OVER() AS total_matching_rows,
  a.id AS appointment_id,
  a.schedule_time,
  COALESCE(
    NULLIF(TRIM(l.full_name), ''),
    NULLIF(TRIM(CONCAT_WS(' ', l.first_name, l.last_name)), ''),
    l.first_name,
    'Unknown Lead'
  ) AS display_name,
  COALESCE(NULLIF(TRIM(a.snapshot_event_name), ''), aet.event_type_name, 'Unknown Event Type') AS event_name,
  CAST(a.snapshot_call_category AS text) AS call_category,
  COALESCE(outcome.name, 'No Outcome') AS outcome_name,
  CASE
    WHEN EXISTS (
      SELECT 1
      FROM fathom_call_records f_check
      WHERE f_check.appointment_id = a.id
        AND f_check.clerk_org_id = a.clerk_org_id
    ) THEN 'Has Fathom Record'
    ELSE 'Missing Fathom Record'
  END AS fathom_status
FROM appointments a
LEFT JOIN leads l
  ON l.id = a.lead_id
 AND l.clerk_org_id = a.clerk_org_id
 AND l.is_deleted = false
LEFT JOIN sales_statuses outcome
  ON outcome.id = a.outcome_id
 AND outcome.clerk_org_id = a.clerk_org_id
LEFT JOIN appointment_event_types aet
  ON aet.id = a.appointment_event_type_id
 AND aet.clerk_org_id = a.clerk_org_id
 AND aet.is_deleted = false
WHERE a.clerk_org_id = :org_id
  AND a.is_deleted = false
  AND a.schedule_time < NOW()
ORDER BY a.schedule_time DESC, a.id ASC
LIMIT :limit

question: Can you give me a clean list of calls with schedule time and outcome?
generated_sql:
SELECT
  COUNT(*) OVER() AS total_matching_rows,
  COALESCE(
    NULLIF(TRIM(l.full_name), ''),
    NULLIF(TRIM(CONCAT_WS(' ', l.first_name, l.last_name)), ''),
    NULLIF(TRIM(l.first_name), ''),
    'Unknown Lead'
  ) AS lead_name,
  COALESCE(
    NULLIF(TRIM(a.snapshot_event_name), ''),
    NULLIF(TRIM(aet.event_type_name), ''),
    'Unknown Event Type'
  ) AS call_name,
  a.schedule_time,
  CASE
    WHEN a.no_show = true THEN 'No Show'
    ELSE COALESCE(NULLIF(TRIM(outcome.name), ''), 'No Outcome')
  END AS outcome_name
FROM appointments a
LEFT JOIN leads l
  ON l.id = a.lead_id
 AND l.clerk_org_id = a.clerk_org_id
 AND l.is_deleted = false
LEFT JOIN sales_statuses outcome
  ON outcome.id = a.outcome_id
 AND outcome.clerk_org_id = a.clerk_org_id
LEFT JOIN appointment_event_types aet
  ON aet.id = a.appointment_event_type_id
 AND aet.clerk_org_id = a.clerk_org_id
 AND aet.is_deleted = false
WHERE a.clerk_org_id = :org_id
  AND a.is_deleted = false
  AND a.schedule_time < NOW()
ORDER BY a.schedule_time DESC, a.id ASC
LIMIT :limit

question: Which appointments were no-shows?
generated_sql:
SELECT
  COUNT(*) OVER() AS total_matching_rows,
  a.id,
  a.schedule_time,
  COALESCE(
    NULLIF(TRIM(l.full_name), ''),
    NULLIF(TRIM(CONCAT_WS(' ', l.first_name, l.last_name)), ''),
    l.first_name,
    'Unknown Lead'
  ) AS display_name,
  COALESCE(NULLIF(TRIM(a.snapshot_event_name), ''), aet.event_type_name, 'Unknown Event Type') AS event_name,
  CAST(a.snapshot_call_category AS text) AS call_category,
  COALESCE(outcome.name, 'No Outcome') AS outcome_name,
  COALESCE(CAST(outcome.role AS text), 'NO_OUTCOME') AS outcome_role,
  a.no_show,
  CAST(a.source AS text) AS appointment_source,
  COALESCE(NULLIF(TRIM(a.host_id), ''), 'No Host') AS host_id,
  COALESCE(NULLIF(TRIM(a.setter_id), ''), 'No Setter') AS setter_id,
  EXISTS (
    SELECT 1
    FROM fathom_call_records f_check
    WHERE f_check.appointment_id = a.id
      AND f_check.clerk_org_id = a.clerk_org_id
  ) AS has_fathom_record
FROM appointments a
LEFT JOIN leads l
  ON l.id = a.lead_id
 AND l.clerk_org_id = a.clerk_org_id
 AND l.is_deleted = false
LEFT JOIN sales_statuses outcome
  ON outcome.id = a.outcome_id
 AND outcome.clerk_org_id = a.clerk_org_id
LEFT JOIN appointment_event_types aet
  ON aet.id = a.appointment_event_type_id
 AND aet.clerk_org_id = a.clerk_org_id
 AND aet.is_deleted = false
WHERE a.clerk_org_id = :org_id
  AND a.is_deleted = false
  AND a.schedule_time < NOW()
  AND a.no_show = true
ORDER BY a.schedule_time DESC, a.id ASC
LIMIT 20;

question: Show me the no-show appointment list.
generated_sql:
SELECT
  COUNT(*) OVER() AS total_matching_rows,
  a.id,
  a.schedule_time,
  COALESCE(
    NULLIF(TRIM(l.full_name), ''),
    NULLIF(TRIM(CONCAT_WS(' ', l.first_name, l.last_name)), ''),
    l.first_name,
    'Unknown Lead'
  ) AS display_name,
  COALESCE(NULLIF(TRIM(a.snapshot_event_name), ''), aet.event_type_name, 'Unknown Event Type') AS event_name,
  CAST(a.snapshot_call_category AS text) AS call_category,
  COALESCE(outcome.name, 'No Outcome') AS outcome_name,
  COALESCE(CAST(outcome.role AS text), 'NO_OUTCOME') AS outcome_role,
  a.no_show,
  CAST(a.source AS text) AS appointment_source,
  COALESCE(NULLIF(TRIM(a.host_id), ''), 'No Host') AS host_id,
  COALESCE(NULLIF(TRIM(a.setter_id), ''), 'No Setter') AS setter_id,
  EXISTS (
    SELECT 1
    FROM fathom_call_records f_check
    WHERE f_check.appointment_id = a.id
      AND f_check.clerk_org_id = a.clerk_org_id
  ) AS has_fathom_record
FROM appointments a
LEFT JOIN leads l
  ON l.id = a.lead_id
 AND l.clerk_org_id = a.clerk_org_id
 AND l.is_deleted = false
LEFT JOIN sales_statuses outcome
  ON outcome.id = a.outcome_id
 AND outcome.clerk_org_id = a.clerk_org_id
LEFT JOIN appointment_event_types aet
  ON aet.id = a.appointment_event_type_id
 AND aet.clerk_org_id = a.clerk_org_id
 AND aet.is_deleted = false
WHERE a.clerk_org_id = :org_id
  AND a.is_deleted = false
  AND a.schedule_time < NOW()
  AND a.no_show = true
ORDER BY a.schedule_time DESC, a.id ASC
LIMIT 20;

question: Which scheduled calls were missed?
generated_sql:
SELECT
  COUNT(*) OVER() AS total_matching_rows,
  a.id,
  a.schedule_time,
  COALESCE(
    NULLIF(TRIM(l.full_name), ''),
    NULLIF(TRIM(CONCAT_WS(' ', l.first_name, l.last_name)), ''),
    l.first_name,
    'Unknown Lead'
  ) AS display_name,
  COALESCE(NULLIF(TRIM(a.snapshot_event_name), ''), aet.event_type_name, 'Unknown Event Type') AS event_name,
  CAST(a.snapshot_call_category AS text) AS call_category,
  COALESCE(outcome.name, 'No Outcome') AS outcome_name,
  COALESCE(CAST(outcome.role AS text), 'NO_OUTCOME') AS outcome_role,
  a.no_show,
  CAST(a.source AS text) AS appointment_source,
  COALESCE(NULLIF(TRIM(a.host_id), ''), 'No Host') AS host_id,
  COALESCE(NULLIF(TRIM(a.setter_id), ''), 'No Setter') AS setter_id,
  EXISTS (
    SELECT 1
    FROM fathom_call_records f_check
    WHERE f_check.appointment_id = a.id
      AND f_check.clerk_org_id = a.clerk_org_id
  ) AS has_fathom_record
FROM appointments a
LEFT JOIN leads l
  ON l.id = a.lead_id
 AND l.clerk_org_id = a.clerk_org_id
 AND l.is_deleted = false
LEFT JOIN sales_statuses outcome
  ON outcome.id = a.outcome_id
 AND outcome.clerk_org_id = a.clerk_org_id
LEFT JOIN appointment_event_types aet
  ON aet.id = a.appointment_event_type_id
 AND aet.clerk_org_id = a.clerk_org_id
 AND aet.is_deleted = false
WHERE a.clerk_org_id = :org_id
  AND a.is_deleted = false
  AND a.schedule_time < NOW()
  AND a.no_show = true
ORDER BY a.schedule_time DESC, a.id ASC
LIMIT :limit

question: Show no-show calls from this month.
generated_sql:
SELECT
  COUNT(*) OVER() AS total_matching_rows,
  a.id,
  a.schedule_time,
  COALESCE(
    NULLIF(TRIM(l.full_name), ''),
    NULLIF(TRIM(CONCAT_WS(' ', l.first_name, l.last_name)), ''),
    l.first_name,
    'Unknown Lead'
  ) AS display_name,
  COALESCE(NULLIF(TRIM(a.snapshot_event_name), ''), aet.event_type_name, 'Unknown Event Type') AS event_name,
  CAST(a.snapshot_call_category AS text) AS call_category,
  COALESCE(outcome.name, 'No Outcome') AS outcome_name,
  COALESCE(CAST(outcome.role AS text), 'NO_OUTCOME') AS outcome_role,
  a.no_show,
  CAST(a.source AS text) AS appointment_source,
  COALESCE(NULLIF(TRIM(a.host_id), ''), 'No Host') AS host_id,
  COALESCE(NULLIF(TRIM(a.setter_id), ''), 'No Setter') AS setter_id,
  EXISTS (
    SELECT 1
    FROM fathom_call_records f_check
    WHERE f_check.appointment_id = a.id
      AND f_check.clerk_org_id = a.clerk_org_id
  ) AS has_fathom_record
FROM appointments a
LEFT JOIN leads l
  ON l.id = a.lead_id
 AND l.clerk_org_id = a.clerk_org_id
 AND l.is_deleted = false
LEFT JOIN sales_statuses outcome
  ON outcome.id = a.outcome_id
 AND outcome.clerk_org_id = a.clerk_org_id
LEFT JOIN appointment_event_types aet
  ON aet.id = a.appointment_event_type_id
 AND aet.clerk_org_id = a.clerk_org_id
 AND aet.is_deleted = false
WHERE a.clerk_org_id = :org_id
  AND a.is_deleted = false
  AND a.schedule_time >= :start_date
  AND a.schedule_time < :end_date
  AND a.schedule_time < NOW()
  AND a.no_show = true
ORDER BY a.schedule_time DESC, a.id ASC
LIMIT :limit;
generated_final_answer: There are no no-show calls so far this month.

question: Give me no-show appointments with host and setter.
generated_sql:
SELECT
  COUNT(*) OVER() AS total_matching_rows,
  a.id AS appointment_id,
  a.schedule_time,
  COALESCE(
    NULLIF(TRIM(l.full_name), ''),
    NULLIF(TRIM(CONCAT_WS(' ', l.first_name, l.last_name)), ''),
    l.first_name,
    'Unknown Lead'
  ) AS display_name,
  COALESCE(NULLIF(TRIM(a.snapshot_event_name), ''), aet.event_type_name, 'Unknown Event Type') AS event_name,
  CAST(a.snapshot_call_category AS text) AS call_category,
  COALESCE(NULLIF(TRIM(a.host_id), ''), 'No Host') AS host_id,
  COALESCE(NULLIF(TRIM(a.setter_id), ''), 'No Setter') AS setter_id
FROM appointments a
LEFT JOIN leads l
  ON l.id = a.lead_id
 AND l.clerk_org_id = a.clerk_org_id
 AND l.is_deleted = false
LEFT JOIN appointment_event_types aet
  ON aet.id = a.appointment_event_type_id
 AND aet.clerk_org_id = a.clerk_org_id
 AND aet.is_deleted = false
WHERE a.clerk_org_id = :org_id
  AND a.is_deleted = false
  AND a.schedule_time < NOW()
  AND a.no_show = true
ORDER BY a.schedule_time DESC, a.id ASC
LIMIT :limit

question: Show no-shows by lead name.
generated_sql:
WITH lead_no_show_stats AS (
  SELECT
    COALESCE(
      NULLIF(TRIM(l.full_name), ''),
      NULLIF(TRIM(CONCAT_WS(' ', l.first_name, l.last_name)), ''),
      l.first_name,
      'Unknown Lead'
    ) AS lead_name,
    COUNT(*) AS total_past_appointments,
    COUNT(*) FILTER (WHERE a.no_show = true) AS no_show_appointments
  FROM appointments a
  LEFT JOIN leads l
    ON l.id = a.lead_id
   AND l.clerk_org_id = a.clerk_org_id
   AND l.is_deleted = false
  WHERE a.clerk_org_id = :org_id
    AND a.is_deleted = false
    AND a.schedule_time < NOW()
  GROUP BY
    COALESCE(
      NULLIF(TRIM(l.full_name), ''),
      NULLIF(TRIM(CONCAT_WS(' ', l.first_name, l.last_name)), ''),
      l.first_name,
      'Unknown Lead'
    )
)
SELECT
  lead_name,
  no_show_appointments,
  total_past_appointments,
  ROUND(100.0 * no_show_appointments / NULLIF(total_past_appointments, 0), 2) AS no_show_rate_percent,
  SUM(no_show_appointments) OVER() AS total_no_show_appointments,
  ROUND(100.0 * no_show_appointments / NULLIF(SUM(no_show_appointments) OVER(), 0), 2) AS percentage_of_total_no_shows
FROM lead_no_show_stats
WHERE no_show_appointments > 0
ORDER BY no_show_appointments DESC, lead_name ASC;

question: List the latest no-show appointments.
generated_sql:
SELECT
  COUNT(*) OVER() AS total_matching_rows,
  a.id,
  a.schedule_time,
  COALESCE(
    NULLIF(TRIM(l.full_name), ''),
    NULLIF(TRIM(CONCAT_WS(' ', l.first_name, l.last_name)), ''),
    l.first_name,
    'Unknown Lead'
  ) AS display_name,
  COALESCE(NULLIF(TRIM(a.snapshot_event_name), ''), aet.event_type_name, 'Unknown Event Type') AS event_name,
  CAST(a.snapshot_call_category AS text) AS call_category,
  COALESCE(outcome.name, 'No Outcome') AS outcome_name,
  COALESCE(CAST(outcome.role AS text), 'NO_OUTCOME') AS outcome_role,
  a.no_show,
  CAST(a.source AS text) AS appointment_source,
  COALESCE(NULLIF(TRIM(a.host_id), ''), 'No Host') AS host_id,
  COALESCE(NULLIF(TRIM(a.setter_id), ''), 'No Setter') AS setter_id,
  EXISTS (
    SELECT 1
    FROM fathom_call_records f_check
    WHERE f_check.appointment_id = a.id
      AND f_check.clerk_org_id = a.clerk_org_id
  ) AS has_fathom_record
FROM appointments a

question: Which Calendly appointments were no-shows?
generated_sql:
SELECT
  COUNT(*) OVER() AS total_matching_rows,
  a.id,
  a.schedule_time,
  COALESCE(
    NULLIF(TRIM(l.full_name), ''),
    NULLIF(TRIM(CONCAT_WS(' ', l.first_name, l.last_name)), ''),
    l.first_name,
    'Unknown Lead'
  ) AS display_name,
  COALESCE(NULLIF(TRIM(a.snapshot_event_name), ''), aet.event_type_name, 'Unknown Event Type') AS event_name,
  CAST(a.snapshot_call_category AS text) AS call_category,
  COALESCE(outcome.name, 'No Outcome') AS outcome_name,
  COALESCE(CAST(outcome.role AS text), 'NO_OUTCOME') AS outcome_role,
  a.no_show,
  CAST(a.source AS text) AS appointment_source,
  COALESCE(NULLIF(TRIM(a.host_id), ''), 'No Host') AS host_id,
  COALESCE(NULLIF(TRIM(a.setter_id), ''), 'No Setter') AS setter_id,
  EXISTS (
    SELECT 1
    FROM fathom_call_records f_check
    WHERE f_check.appointment_id = a.id
      AND f_check.clerk_org_id = a.clerk_org_id
  ) AS has_fathom_record
FROM appointments a
LEFT JOIN leads l
  ON l.id = a.lead_id
 AND l.clerk_org_id = a.clerk_org_id
 AND l.is_deleted = false
LEFT JOIN sales_statuses outcome
  ON outcome.id = a.outcome_id
 AND outcome.clerk_org_id = a.clerk_org_id
LEFT JOIN appointment_event_types aet
  ON aet.id = a.appointment_event_type_id
 AND aet.clerk_org_id = a.clerk_org_id
 AND aet.is_deleted = false
WHERE a.clerk_org_id = :org_id
  AND a.is_deleted = false
  AND a.schedule_time < NOW()
  AND a.no_show = true
  AND a.source = :appointment_source
ORDER BY a.schedule_time DESC, a.id ASC
LIMIT :limit

question: Which manual appointments were no-shows?
generated_sql:
SELECT
  COUNT(*) OVER() AS total_matching_rows,
  a.id,
  a.schedule_time,
  COALESCE(
    NULLIF(TRIM(l.full_name), ''),
    NULLIF(TRIM(CONCAT_WS(' ', l.first_name, l.last_name)), ''),
    l.first_name,
    'Unknown Lead'
  ) AS display_name,
  COALESCE(NULLIF(TRIM(a.snapshot_event_name), ''), aet.event_type_name, 'Unknown Event Type') AS event_name,
  CAST(a.snapshot_call_category AS text) AS call_category,
  COALESCE(outcome.name, 'No Outcome') AS outcome_name,
  COALESCE(CAST(outcome.role AS text), 'NO_OUTCOME') AS outcome_role,
  a.no_show,
  CAST(a.source AS text) AS appointment_source,
  COALESCE(NULLIF(TRIM(a.host_id), ''), 'No Host') AS host_id,
  COALESCE(NULLIF(TRIM(a.setter_id), ''), 'No Setter') AS setter_id,
  EXISTS (
    SELECT 1
    FROM fathom_call_records f_check
    WHERE f_check.appointment_id = a.id
      AND f_check.clerk_org_id = a.clerk_org_id
  ) AS has_fathom_record
FROM appointments a
LEFT JOIN leads l
  ON l.id = a.lead_id
 AND l.clerk_org_id = a.clerk_org_id
 AND l.is_deleted = false
LEFT JOIN sales_statuses outcome
  ON outcome.id = a.outcome_id
 AND outcome.clerk_org_id = a.clerk_org_id
LEFT JOIN appointment_event_types aet
  ON aet.id = a.appointment_event_type_id
 AND aet.clerk_org_id = a.clerk_org_id
 AND aet.is_deleted = false
WHERE a.clerk_org_id = :org_id
  AND a.is_deleted = false
  AND a.schedule_time < NOW()
  AND a.no_show = true
  AND a.source = 'MANUAL'
ORDER BY a.schedule_time DESC, a.id ASC
LIMIT :limit
generated_final_answer: There are no manual appointments marked as no-shows.

question: Give me a list of missed appointments for the selected period.

SELECT
  COUNT(*) OVER() AS total_matching_rows,
  a.id,
  a.schedule_time,
  COALESCE(

    NULLIF(TRIM(CONCAT_WS(' ', l.first_name, l.last_name)), ''),
    l.first_name,
    'Unknown Lead'
  ) AS display_name,
  COALESCE(NULLIF(TRIM(a.snapshot_event_name), ''), aet.event_type_name, 'Unknown Event Type') AS event_name,
  CAST(a.snapshot_call_category AS text) AS call_category,
  COALESCE(outcome.name, 'No Outcome') AS outcome_name,
  COALESCE(CAST(outcome.role AS text), 'NO_OUTCOME') AS outcome_role,
  a.no_show,
  CAST(a.source AS text) AS appointment_source,
  COALESCE(NULLIF(TRIM(a.host_id), ''), 'No Host') AS host_id,
  COALESCE(NULLIF(TRIM(a.setter_id), ''), 'No Setter') AS setter_id,
  EXISTS (
    SELECT 1
    FROM fathom_call_records f_check
    WHERE f_check.appointment_id = a.id
      AND f_check.clerk_org_id = a.clerk_org_id
  ) AS has_fathom_record
FROM appointments a
LEFT JOIN leads l
  ON l.id = a.lead_id
 AND l.clerk_org_id = a.clerk_org_id
 AND l.is_deleted = false
LEFT JOIN sales_statuses outcome
  ON outcome.id = a.outcome_id
 AND outcome.clerk_org_id = a.clerk_org_id
LEFT JOIN appointment_event_types aet
  ON aet.id = a.appointment_event_type_id
 AND aet.clerk_org_id = a.clerk_org_id
 AND aet.is_deleted = false
WHERE a.clerk_org_id = :org_id
  AND a.is_deleted = false
  AND a.schedule_time >= :start_date
  AND a.schedule_time < :end_date
  AND a.schedule_time < NOW()
  AND a.no_show = true
ORDER BY a.schedule_time DESC, a.id ASC
LIMIT 20

question: What is the Fathom coverage for past appointments?
notes: For coverage, use past appointments by default; future appointments should not be counted as missing Fathom.
generated_sql:
SELECT
  COUNT(DISTINCT a.id) AS total_past_appointments,
  COUNT(DISTINCT a.id) FILTER (WHERE f.id IS NOT NULL) AS appointments_with_fathom,
  COUNT(DISTINCT a.id) FILTER (WHERE f.id IS NULL) AS appointments_without_fathom,
  ROUND(
    100.0 * COUNT(DISTINCT a.id) FILTER (WHERE f.id IS NOT NULL) / NULLIF(COUNT(DISTINCT a.id), 0),
    2
  ) AS fathom_coverage_percent
FROM appointments a
LEFT JOIN fathom_call_records f
  ON f.appointment_id = a.id
 AND f.clerk_org_id = a.clerk_org_id
WHERE a.clerk_org_id = :org_id
  AND a.is_deleted = false
  AND a.schedule_time < NOW();
generated_final_answer: Out of 449 past appointments, 82 have a Fathom record and 367 do not. Fathom coverage for past appointments is 18.26%.

question: What is Fathom coverage by event type?
notes: For coverage, use past appointments by default; future appointments should not be counted as missing Fathom.
generated_sql:
WITH past_appointments AS (
  SELECT
    a.id,
    COALESCE(NULLIF(TRIM(a.snapshot_event_name), ''), aet.event_type_name, 'Unknown Event Type') AS event_name,
    EXISTS (
      SELECT 1
      FROM fathom_call_records f
      WHERE f.appointment_id = a.id
        AND f.clerk_org_id = a.clerk_org_id
    ) AS has_fathom_record
  FROM appointments a
  LEFT JOIN appointment_event_types aet
    ON aet.id = a.appointment_event_type_id
   AND aet.clerk_org_id = a.clerk_org_id
   AND aet.is_deleted = false
  WHERE a.clerk_org_id = :org_id
    AND a.is_deleted = false
    AND a.schedule_time < NOW()
)
SELECT
  event_name,
  COUNT(*) AS past_appointments,
  COUNT(*) FILTER (WHERE has_fathom_record) AS appointments_with_fathom,
  COUNT(*) FILTER (WHERE NOT has_fathom_record) AS appointments_without_fathom,
  ROUND(COUNT(*) * 100.0 / NULLIF(SUM(COUNT(*)) OVER(), 0), 2) AS percentage_of_total_past_appointments,
  ROUND(
    100.0 * COUNT(*) FILTER (WHERE has_fathom_record) / NULLIF(COUNT(*), 0),
    2
  ) AS fathom_coverage_percent,
  SUM(COUNT(*)) OVER() AS total_matching_past_appointments
FROM past_appointments
GROUP BY event_name
ORDER BY fathom_coverage_percent DESC NULLS LAST, past_appointments DESC, event_name ASC;

question: Which call category has the best Fathom coverage?
notes: For coverage, use past appointments by default; future appointments should not be counted as missing Fathom.
generated_sql:
WITH past_appointments AS (
  SELECT
    a.id,
    COALESCE(CAST(a.snapshot_call_category AS text), 'UNKNOWN') AS call_category,
    EXISTS (
      SELECT 1
      FROM fathom_call_records f
      WHERE f.appointment_id = a.id
        AND f.clerk_org_id = a.clerk_org_id
    ) AS has_fathom_record
  FROM appointments a
  WHERE a.clerk_org_id = :org_id
    AND a.is_deleted = false
    AND a.schedule_time < NOW()
),
category_coverage AS (
  SELECT
    call_category,
    COUNT(*) AS total_past_appointments,
    COUNT(*) FILTER (WHERE has_fathom_record) AS appointments_with_fathom,
    COUNT(*) FILTER (WHERE NOT has_fathom_record) AS appointments_without_fathom,
    ROUND(
      100.0 * COUNT(*) FILTER (WHERE has_fathom_record) / NULLIF(COUNT(*), 0),
      2
    ) AS fathom_coverage_percent
  FROM past_appointments
  GROUP BY call_category
)
SELECT
  call_category,
  total_past_appointments,
  appointments_with_fathom,
  appointments_without_fathom,
  fathom_coverage_percent,
  SUM(total_past_appointments) OVER() AS total_matching_past_appointments
FROM category_coverage
ORDER BY fathom_coverage_percent DESC NULLS LAST, total_past_appointments DESC, call_category ASC;
generated_final_answer: Sales Call has the best Fathom coverage at 18.26% — 82 of 449 past appointments have a Fathom record.

question: Which appointments have no Fathom call record?
generated_sql:
SELECT
  COUNT(*) OVER() AS total_matching_rows,
  a.id,
  a.schedule_time,
  COALESCE(
    NULLIF(TRIM(l.full_name), ''),
    NULLIF(TRIM(CONCAT_WS(' ', l.first_name, l.last_name)), ''),
    l.first_name,
    'Unknown Lead'
  ) AS display_name,
  COALESCE(NULLIF(TRIM(a.snapshot_event_name), ''), aet.event_type_name, 'Unknown Event Type') AS event_name,
  CAST(a.snapshot_call_category AS text) AS call_category,
  COALESCE(outcome.name, 'No Outcome') AS outcome_name,
  COALESCE(CAST(outcome.role AS text), 'NO_OUTCOME') AS outcome_role,
  a.no_show,
  CAST(a.source AS text) AS appointment_source,
  COALESCE(NULLIF(TRIM(a.host_id), ''), 'No Host') AS host_id,
  COALESCE(NULLIF(TRIM(a.setter_id), ''), 'No Setter') AS setter_id,
  false AS has_fathom_record
FROM appointments a
LEFT JOIN leads l
  ON l.id = a.lead_id
 AND l.clerk_org_id = a.clerk_org_id
 AND l.is_deleted = false
LEFT JOIN sales_statuses outcome
  ON outcome.id = a.outcome_id
 AND outcome.clerk_org_id = a.clerk_org_id
LEFT JOIN appointment_event_types aet
  ON aet.id = a.appointment_event_type_id
 AND aet.clerk_org_id = a.clerk_org_id
 AND aet.is_deleted = false
WHERE a.clerk_org_id = :org_id
  AND a.is_deleted = false
  AND a.schedule_time < NOW()
  AND NOT EXISTS (
    SELECT 1
    FROM fathom_call_records f_check
    WHERE f_check.appointment_id = a.id
      AND f_check.clerk_org_id = a.clerk_org_id
  )
ORDER BY a.schedule_time DESC, a.id ASC
LIMIT 20;

SELECT
  COUNT(*) OVER() AS total_matching_rows,
  a.id,
  a.schedule_time,
  COALESCE(
    NULLIF(TRIM(l.full_name), ''),
    NULLIF(TRIM(CONCAT_WS(' ', l.first_name, l.last_name)), ''),
    l.first_name,
    'Unknown Lead'
  ) AS display_name,
  COALESCE(NULLIF(TRIM(a.snapshot_event_name), ''), aet.event_type_name, 'Unknown Event Type') AS event_name,
  CAST(a.snapshot_call_category AS text) AS call_category,
  COALESCE(outcome.name, 'No Outcome') AS outcome_name,
  COALESCE(CAST(outcome.role AS text), 'NO_OUTCOME') AS outcome_role,
  a.no_show,
  CAST(a.source AS text) AS appointment_source,
  COALESCE(NULLIF(TRIM(a.host_id), ''), 'No Host') AS host_id,
  COALESCE(NULLIF(TRIM(a.setter_id), ''), 'No Setter') AS setter_id,
  false AS has_fathom_record
FROM appointments a
LEFT JOIN leads l
  ON l.id = a.lead_id
 AND l.clerk_org_id = a.clerk_org_id
 AND l.is_deleted = false
LEFT JOIN sales_statuses outcome
  ON outcome.id = a.outcome_id
 AND outcome.clerk_org_id = a.clerk_org_id
LEFT JOIN appointment_event_types aet

question: Which completed calls were not captured by Fathom?
generated_sql:
SELECT
  COUNT(*) OVER() AS total_matching_rows,
  a.id,
  a.schedule_time,
  COALESCE(
    NULLIF(TRIM(l.full_name), ''),
    NULLIF(TRIM(CONCAT_WS(' ', l.first_name, l.last_name)), ''),
    l.first_name,
    'Unknown Lead'
  ) AS display_name,
  COALESCE(NULLIF(TRIM(a.snapshot_event_name), ''), aet.event_type_name, 'Unknown Event Type') AS event_name,
  CAST(a.snapshot_call_category AS text) AS call_category,
  COALESCE(outcome.name, 'No Outcome') AS outcome_name,
  COALESCE(CAST(outcome.role AS text), 'NO_OUTCOME') AS outcome_role,
  CAST(a.source AS text) AS appointment_source,
  COALESCE(NULLIF(TRIM(a.host_id), ''), 'No Host') AS host_id,
  COALESCE(NULLIF(TRIM(a.setter_id), ''), 'No Setter') AS setter_id,
  false AS has_fathom_record
FROM appointments a
LEFT JOIN leads l
  ON l.id = a.lead_id
 AND l.clerk_org_id = a.clerk_org_id
 AND l.is_deleted = false
LEFT JOIN sales_statuses outcome
  ON outcome.id = a.outcome_id
 AND outcome.clerk_org_id = a.clerk_org_id
LEFT JOIN appointment_event_types aet
  ON aet.id = a.appointment_event_type_id
 AND aet.clerk_org_id = a.clerk_org_id
 AND aet.is_deleted = false
WHERE a.clerk_org_id = :org_id
  AND a.is_deleted = false
  AND a.schedule_time < NOW()
  AND a.no_show = false
  AND NOT EXISTS (
    SELECT 1
    FROM fathom_call_records f_check
    WHERE f_check.appointment_id = a.id
      AND f_check.clerk_org_id = a.clerk_org_id
  )
ORDER BY a.schedule_time DESC, a.id ASC
LIMIT 20

question: Show appointments missing Fathom for this month.
generated_sql:
SELECT
  COUNT(*) OVER() AS total_matching_rows,
  a.id,
  a.schedule_time,
  COALESCE(
    NULLIF(TRIM(l.full_name), ''),
    NULLIF(TRIM(CONCAT_WS(' ', l.first_name, l.last_name)), ''),
    l.first_name,
    'Unknown Lead'
  ) AS display_name,
  COALESCE(NULLIF(TRIM(a.snapshot_event_name), ''), aet.event_type_name, 'Unknown Event Type') AS event_name,
  CAST(a.snapshot_call_category AS text) AS call_category,
  COALESCE(outcome.name, 'No Outcome') AS outcome_name,
  COALESCE(CAST(outcome.role AS text), 'NO_OUTCOME') AS outcome_role,
  a.no_show,
  CAST(a.source AS text) AS appointment_source,
  COALESCE(NULLIF(TRIM(a.host_id), ''), 'No Host') AS host_id,
  COALESCE(NULLIF(TRIM(a.setter_id), ''), 'No Setter') AS setter_id,
  false AS has_fathom_record
FROM appointments a
LEFT JOIN leads l
  ON l.id = a.lead_id
 AND l.clerk_org_id = a.clerk_org_id
 AND l.is_deleted = false
LEFT JOIN sales_statuses outcome
  ON outcome.id = a.outcome_id
 AND outcome.clerk_org_id = a.clerk_org_id
LEFT JOIN appointment_event_types aet
  ON aet.id = a.appointment_event_type_id
 AND aet.clerk_org_id = a.clerk_org_id
 AND aet.is_deleted = false
WHERE a.clerk_org_id = :org_id
  AND a.is_deleted = false
  AND a.schedule_time >= :start_date
  AND a.schedule_time < :end_date
  AND a.schedule_time < NOW()
  AND NOT EXISTS (
    SELECT 1
    FROM fathom_call_records f_check
    WHERE f_check.appointment_id = a.id
      AND f_check.clerk_org_id = a.clerk_org_id
  )
ORDER BY a.schedule_time DESC, a.id ASC
LIMIT :limit

question: List calls without Fathom summaries.
generated_sql:
SELECT
  COUNT(*) OVER() AS total_matching_rows,
  a.id,
  a.schedule_time,
  COALESCE(
    NULLIF(TRIM(l.full_name), ''),
    NULLIF(TRIM(CONCAT_WS(' ', l.first_name, l.last_name)), ''),
    l.first_name,
    'Unknown Lead'
  ) AS display_name,
  COALESCE(NULLIF(TRIM(a.snapshot_event_name), ''), aet.event_type_name, 'Unknown Event Type') AS event_name,
  CAST(a.snapshot_call_category AS text) AS call_category,
  COALESCE(outcome.name, 'No Outcome') AS outcome_name,
  COALESCE(CAST(outcome.role AS text), 'NO_OUTCOME') AS outcome_role,
  a.no_show,
  CAST(a.source AS text) AS appointment_source,
  COALESCE(NULLIF(TRIM(a.host_id), ''), 'No Host') AS host_id,
  COALESCE(NULLIF(TRIM(a.setter_id), ''), 'No Setter') AS setter_id,
  EXISTS (
    SELECT 1
    FROM fathom_call_records f_check
    WHERE f_check.appointment_id = a.id
      AND f_check.clerk_org_id = a.clerk_org_id
  ) AS has_fathom_record,
  CASE
    WHEN EXISTS (
      SELECT 1
      FROM fathom_call_records f_any
      WHERE f_any.appointment_id = a.id
        AND f_any.clerk_org_id = a.clerk_org_id
    ) THEN 'Fathom record present but summary missing'
    ELSE 'No Fathom record'
  END AS missing_summary_reason
FROM appointments a
LEFT JOIN leads l
  ON l.id = a.lead_id
 AND l.clerk_org_id = a.clerk_org_id
 AND l.is_deleted = false
LEFT JOIN sales_statuses outcome
  ON outcome.id = a.outcome_id
 AND outcome.clerk_org_id = a.clerk_org_id
LEFT JOIN appointment_event_types aet
  ON aet.id = a.appointment_event_type_id
 AND aet.clerk_org_id = a.clerk_org_id
 AND aet.is_deleted = false
WHERE a.clerk_org_id = :org_id
  AND a.is_deleted = false
  AND a.schedule_time < NOW()
  AND NOT EXISTS (
    SELECT 1
    FROM fathom_call_records f_summary
    WHERE f_summary.appointment_id = a.id
      AND f_summary.clerk_org_id = a.clerk_org_id
      AND NULLIF(TRIM(f_summary.summary), '') IS NOT NULL
  )
ORDER BY a.schedule_time DESC, a.id ASC
LIMIT 20;

question: Which sales calls have no Fathom record?
generated_sql:
SELECT
  COUNT(*) OVER() AS total_matching_rows,
  a.id,
  a.schedule_time,
  COALESCE(
    NULLIF(TRIM(l.full_name), ''),
    NULLIF(TRIM(CONCAT_WS(' ', l.first_name, l.last_name)), ''),
    l.first_name,
    'Unknown Lead'
  ) AS display_name,
  COALESCE(NULLIF(TRIM(a.snapshot_event_name), ''), aet.event_type_name, 'Unknown Event Type') AS event_name,
  CAST(a.snapshot_call_category AS text) AS call_category,
  COALESCE(outcome.name, 'No Outcome') AS outcome_name,
  COALESCE(CAST(outcome.role AS text), 'NO_OUTCOME') AS outcome_role,
  a.no_show,
  CAST(a.source AS text) AS appointment_source,
  COALESCE(NULLIF(TRIM(a.host_id), ''), 'No Host') AS host_id,
  COALESCE(NULLIF(TRIM(a.setter_id), ''), 'No Setter') AS setter_id,
  false AS has_fathom_record
FROM appointments a
LEFT JOIN leads l
  ON l.id = a.lead_id
 AND l.clerk_org_id = a.clerk_org_id
 AND l.is_deleted = false
LEFT JOIN sales_statuses outcome
  ON outcome.id = a.outcome_id
 AND outcome.clerk_org_id = a.clerk_org_id
LEFT JOIN appointment_event_types aet
  ON aet.id = a.appointment_event_type_id
 AND aet.clerk_org_id = a.clerk_org_id
 AND aet.is_deleted = false

ORDER BY a.schedule_time DESC, a.id ASC

  COUNT(*) AS appointments_without_fathom,
  SUM(COUNT(*)) OVER() AS total_matching_appointments,
  ROUND(
    COUNT(*) * 100.0 / NULLIF(SUM(COUNT(*)) OVER(), 0),
    2
  ) AS percentage_of_total
FROM appointments a
WHERE a.clerk_org_id = :org_id
  AND a.is_deleted = false
  AND a.schedule_time < NOW()
  AND NOT EXISTS (
    SELECT 1
    FROM fathom_call_records f_check
    WHERE f_check.appointment_id = a.id
      AND f_check.clerk_org_id = a.clerk_org_id
  )
GROUP BY COALESCE(NULLIF(TRIM(a.setter_id), ''), 'No Setter')
ORDER BY appointments_without_fathom DESC, setter_id ASC;

question: Which event types have appointments missing Fathom?
generated_sql:
WITH event_type_fathom_coverage AS (
  SELECT
    COALESCE(NULLIF(TRIM(a.snapshot_event_name), ''), aet.event_type_name, 'Unknown Event Type') AS event_name,
    COUNT(DISTINCT a.id) AS total_past_appointments,
    COUNT(DISTINCT a.id) FILTER (WHERE f.id IS NULL) AS appointments_missing_fathom
  FROM appointments a
  LEFT JOIN appointment_event_types aet
    ON aet.id = a.appointment_event_type_id
   AND aet.clerk_org_id = a.clerk_org_id
   AND aet.is_deleted = false
  LEFT JOIN fathom_call_records f
    ON f.appointment_id = a.id
   AND f.clerk_org_id = a.clerk_org_id
  WHERE a.clerk_org_id = :org_id
    AND a.is_deleted = false
    AND a.schedule_time < NOW()
  GROUP BY COALESCE(NULLIF(TRIM(a.snapshot_event_name), ''), aet.event_type_name, 'Unknown Event Type')
)
SELECT
  event_name,
  appointments_missing_fathom,
  total_past_appointments,
  ROUND(
    100.0 * appointments_missing_fathom / NULLIF(total_past_appointments, 0),
    2
  ) AS missing_fathom_rate_percent,
  SUM(appointments_missing_fathom) OVER() AS total_matching_appointments_missing_fathom,
  ROUND(
    appointments_missing_fathom * 100.0 / NULLIF(SUM(appointments_missing_fathom) OVER(), 0),
    2
  ) AS percentage_of_total_missing_fathom
FROM event_type_fathom_coverage
WHERE appointments_missing_fathom > 0
ORDER BY appointments_missing_fathom DESC, event_name ASC;

question: Can you list appointments that should have Fathom but do not?
generated_sql:
SELECT
  COUNT(*) OVER() AS total_matching_rows,
  a.id,
  a.schedule_time,
  COALESCE(
    NULLIF(TRIM(l.full_name), ''),
    NULLIF(TRIM(CONCAT_WS(' ', l.first_name, l.last_name)), ''),
    l.first_name,
    'Unknown Lead'
  ) AS display_name,
  COALESCE(NULLIF(TRIM(a.snapshot_event_name), ''), aet.event_type_name, 'Unknown Event Type') AS event_name,
  CAST(a.snapshot_call_category AS text) AS call_category,
  COALESCE(outcome.name, 'No Outcome') AS outcome_name,
  COALESCE(CAST(outcome.role AS text), 'NO_OUTCOME') AS outcome_role,
  a.no_show,
  CAST(a.source AS text) AS appointment_source,
  COALESCE(NULLIF(TRIM(a.host_id), ''), 'No Host') AS host_id,
  COALESCE(NULLIF(TRIM(a.setter_id), ''), 'No Setter') AS setter_id,
  false AS has_fathom_record
FROM appointments a
LEFT JOIN leads l
  ON l.id = a.lead_id
 AND l.clerk_org_id = a.clerk_org_id
 AND l.is_deleted = false
LEFT JOIN sales_statuses outcome
  ON outcome.id = a.outcome_id
 AND outcome.clerk_org_id = a.clerk_org_id
LEFT JOIN appointment_event_types aet
  ON aet.id = a.appointment_event_type_id
 AND aet.clerk_org_id = a.clerk_org_id
 AND aet.is_deleted = false
WHERE a.clerk_org_id = :org_id
  AND a.is_deleted = false
  AND a.schedule_time < NOW()
  AND NOT EXISTS (
    SELECT 1
    FROM fathom_call_records f_check
    WHERE f_check.appointment_id = a.id
      AND f_check.clerk_org_id = a.clerk_org_id
  )
ORDER BY a.schedule_time DESC, a.id ASC
LIMIT 20;

SELECT
  COUNT(*) OVER() AS total_matching_rows,
  a.id,
